mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in labor costs signals an expansionary fiscal policy response that compresses aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.

A decline in aggregate supply signals a contractionary monetary policy response that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.

A sustained increase in the price level signals an inflationary monetary policy response that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.

A decline in aggregate supply signals a contractionary fiscal policy response that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
A sustained increase in labor costs signals an expansionary fiscal policy response that compresses aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.

A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy response that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
A sustained increase in labor costs signals an expansionary fiscal policy response that compresses aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.

A decline in aggregate supply signals a contractionary fiscal policy response that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
